name: BTC 1h Session Impulse De-Risk v1
edge_mechanism: a session impulse establishes directional bias, but the trade de-risks quickly if follow-through does not appear soon after entry
thesis: The cycle asks for session-window continuation ideas, and BTC 1h is the best lane in the allowed basket for that concept because it carries more directional transition than ETH 1h while remaining liquid and structured. The edge is not a heroic opening-range breakout. It is a session impulse plus fast failure recognition when continuation does not build.
invalidation_condition: If session impulses do not show enough follow-through, or if the de-risk rule cuts nearly every trade before any edge can emerge, the mechanism fails.

pre_submit_reasoning.market_behavior_targeted: A session begins with directional impulse, structure holds, and the move either continues quickly or is exited early as a failed impulse.
pre_submit_reasoning.why_edge_should_exist: Transitionary 1h structure can reward early alignment with session direction, but only if the trade admits failure fast. That combination makes continuation more plausible than on pure-chop AXS while avoiding late breakout chasing.
pre_submit_reasoning.asset_timeframe_fit: BTC 1h is the best fit in the basket for this mechanism because it has the liquidity and directional transitions to make session impulses more meaningful than on ETH 4h or AXS 1h.
pre_submit_reasoning.likely_failure_mode: The market may remain rotational despite early session impulse, causing continuation entries to fade back into range quickly.
pre_submit_reasoning.first_refinement_path: If signals are present but too many die immediately, tighten the hold requirement around EMA_20 before widening the impulse threshold.
pre_submit_reasoning.expected_outcome_pattern: A moderate number of trades with many small scratches or reduced-risk exits, and a smaller number of meaningful continuation winners.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_success: BTC 1h produces credible session-impulse density, the fast de-risk rule limits failed continuation losses, and ETH 1h shows a weaker but similar pattern.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_failure: 0 trades, sub-10 trade screen output, or de-risking every trade before any continuation edge materializes.

density_sanity_check.event_sparsity: moderate but acceptable because session impulse plus simple hold is denser than earlier persistence chains

test_lanes.why_included: Best fit for a session-impulse continuation concept inside the allowed basket.
test_lanes.continue_if: BTC 1h shows enough impulse-follow-through sequences and the fast de-risk rule contains failed starts.
test_lanes.rotate_away_if: BTC 1h remains too range-bound for session continuation to survive.
test_lanes.expected_if_right: Moderate participation with a mix of scratches and occasional larger continuation legs.

test_lanes.why_included: Checks whether the cycle's primary lane can still express a milder session-impulse continuation pattern.
test_lanes.continue_if: ETH 1h preserves the same impulse-then-hold logic with smaller moves.
test_lanes.rotate_away_if: ETH 1h stays too choppy for session continuation.
test_lanes.expected_if_right: Lower amplitude and lower follow-through than BTC.

test_lanes.why_included: Stress-tests whether the same session logic survives on a noisier asset.
test_lanes.continue_if: AXS 1h preserves session impulse behavior with acceptable noise.
test_lanes.rotate_away_if: AXS 1h turns the concept into random spikes.
test_lanes.expected_if_right: Messier but sometimes sharper continuation bursts.

long: De-risk to break-even if no 0.25 * ATR_14 favorable move within 2 bars
short: De-risk to break-even if no 0.25 * ATR_14 favorable move within 2 bars
